summary: Timezone bug fix VERIFIED. Backend returns UTC ISO format with 'Z' suffix for all appointments. Frontend uses unified formatDateTimeFr() utility. Both invitation page and appointment detail page now show IDENTICAL date/time for the same appointment. All 13 backend tests passed, all frontend UI tests passed.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/invitations/{token} returns start_datetime in UTC with Z suffix (PASSED)
Backend: Second invitation token returns UTC format (PASSED)
Backend: GET /api/appointments/ returns all start_datetime in UTC with Z suffix (PASSED)
Backend: GET /api/appointments/{id} returns start_datetime in UTC with Z suffix (PASSED)
Backend: Invitation and Appointment APIs return IDENTICAL datetime for same appointment (PASSED - CRITICAL)
Backend: normalize_to_utc() handles UTC strings correctly (PASSED)
Backend: normalize_to_utc() handles naive datetime as Europe/Paris (PASSED)
Backend: normalize_to_utc() handles offset datetime correctly (PASSED)
Backend: dateFormat.js exists with all required functions (PASSED)
Frontend: Invitation page loads and displays date 'dimanche 22 mars 2026 à 00:04' (PASSED)
Frontend: Appointment detail page displays SAME date 'dimanche 22 mars 2026 à 00:04' (PASSED - CRITICAL)
Frontend: Second invitation displays correct UTC time '12:30' (PASSED)
Frontend: Dashboard displays appointments with correct dates (PASSED)
critical_code_review_comments: Backend date_utils.py correctly implements normalize_to_utc() that converts naive datetimes (interpreted as Europe/Paris) to UTC
Backend appointments.py and invitations.py both call normalize_to_utc() on start_datetime before returning
Frontend dateFormat.js is the SINGLE source of truth for date formatting, uses Intl.DateTimeFormat with USER_TIMEZONE
InvitationPage.js removed old hack of appending 'Z' to naive strings, now uses formatDateTimeFr(appointment.start_datetime)
OrganizerDashboard.js uses formatDateTimeCompactFr() and parseUTC() for appointment cards
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - used existing test appointments
context_for_next_testing_agent: Timezone bug fix fully verified. Key changes: 1) Backend normalizes all dates to UTC ISO format with 'Z' suffix via normalize_to_utc(), 2) Frontend uses unified dateFormat.js utility with formatDateTimeFr() for all date displays, 3) Old InvitationPage.js hack of appending 'Z' to naive strings removed. Both invitation page and appointment detail page now show IDENTICAL date/time for the same appointment.

verified_features.backend_utc_format: All API endpoints return start_datetime in UTC ISO format with 'Z' suffix
verified_features.legacy_normalization: normalize_to_utc() correctly interprets naive datetimes as Europe/Paris and converts to UTC
verified_features.frontend_unified_formatting: dateFormat.js is single source of truth, uses Intl.DateTimeFormat with user's timezone
verified_features.date_consistency: CRITICAL: Invitation page and Appointment detail page show IDENTICAL date/time for same appointment
verified_features.invitation_page_fix: Removed old '+ Z' hack, now uses formatDateTimeFr(appointment.start_datetime)
verified_features.dashboard_dates: OrganizerDashboard correctly displays appointment dates using formatDateTimeCompactFr()

bug_fix_verification.original_bug: Organizer page showed 01:04 while Invitation page showed 00:04 for the same appointment
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: Inconsistent date handling - backend returned naive datetime strings, frontend had inconsistent timezone handling
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Backend now stores/returns ALL dates in UTC ISO format with 'Z' suffix. Frontend uses unified formatDateTimeFr() utility that converts UTC to user's local timezone via Intl.DateTimeFormat
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: PASSED - Both pages now show identical date/time: 'dimanche 22 mars 2026 à 00:04'
